author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again, on Matt. vi. on the Lord’s Prayer. To the Competentes.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Again, on Matt. vi. on the Lord’s Prayer. To the Competentes.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The order established for your edification requires that ye learn first what to believe, and afterwards what to ask.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so saith the Apostle, “Whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord, shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This testimony blessed Paul cited out of the Prophet; for by the Prophet were those times foretold, when all men should call upon God; “Whosoever shall call upon the Name of the Lord, shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he added, “How then shall they call on Him in whom they have not believed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how shall they believe in Him of whom they have not heard?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or how shall they hear without a preacher?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or how shall they preach except they be sent?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore were preachers sent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They preached Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As they preached, the people heard, by hearing they believed, and by believing called upon Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then it was most rightly and most truly said, “How shall they call on Him in whom they have not believed?” therefore have ye first learned what to believe: and to-day have learnt to call on Him in whom ye have believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Son of God, our Lord Jesus Christ, hath taught us a Prayer; and though He be the Lord Himself, as ye have heard and repeated in the Creed, the Only Son of God, yet He would not be alone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is the Only Son, and yet would not be alone; He hath vouchsafed to have brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to whom doth He say, “Say, Our Father, which art in heaven?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whom did He wish us to call our Father, save His own Father?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did He grudge us this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Parents sometimes when they have gotten one, or two, or three children, fear to give birth to any more, lest they reduce the rest to beggary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because the inheritance which He promiseth us is such as many may possess, and no one be straitened; therefore hath He called into His brotherhood the peoples of the nations; and the Only Son hath numberless brethren; who say, “Our Father, which art in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So said they who have been before us; and so shall say those who will come after us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how many brethren the Only Son hath in His grace, sharing His inheritance with those for whom He suffered death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We had a father and mother on earth, that we might be born to labours and to death: but we have found other parents, God our Father, and the Church our Mother, by whom we are born unto life eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then consider, beloved, whose children we have begun to be; and let us live so as becomes those who have such a Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, how that our Creator hath condescended to be our Father!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard whom we ought to call upon, and with what hope of an eternal inheritance we have begun to have a Father in heaven; let us now hear what we must ask of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of such a Father what shall we ask?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do we not ask rain of Him, to-day, and yesterday, and the day before?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is no great thing to have asked of such a Father, and yet ye see with what sighings, and with what great desire we ask for rain, when death is feared, when that is feared which none can escape.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For sooner or later every man must die, and we groan, and pray, and travail in pain, and cry to God, that we may die a little later.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much more ought we to cry to Him, that we may come to that place where we shall never die!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore is it said, “Hallowed be Thy Name.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This we also ask of Him that his Name may be hallowed in us; for Holy is it always.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how is His Name hallowed in us, except while it makes us holy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For once we were not holy, and we are made holy by His Name; but He is always Holy, and His Name always Holy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is for ourselves, not for God, that we pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we do not wish well to God, to whom no ill can ever happen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we wish what is good for ourselves, that His Holy Name may be hallowed, that that which is always Holy, may be hallowed in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy kingdom come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come it surely will, whether we ask or no.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Indeed, God hath an eternal kingdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when did He not reign?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When did He begin to reign?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For His kingdom hath no beginning, neither shall it have any end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that we may know that in this prayer also we pray for ourselves, and not for God (for we do not say, “Thy kingdom come,” as though we were asking that God may reign); we shall be ourselves His kingdom, if believing in Him we make progress in this faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the faithful, redeemed by the Blood of His Only Son, will be His kingdom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this His kingdom will come, when the resurrection of the dead shall have taken place; for then He will come Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when the dead are risen, He will divide them, as He Himself saith, “and He shall set some on the right hand, and some on the left.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To those who shall be on the right hand He will say, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is what we wish and pray for when we say, “Thy kingdom come;” that it may come to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we shall be reprobates, that kingdom will come to others, but not to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we shall be of that number, who belong to the members of His Only-begotten Son, His kingdom will come to us, and will not tarry.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For are there as many ages yet remaining, as have already passed away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle John hath said, “My little children, it is the last hour.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is a long hour proportioned to this long day; and see how many years this last hour lasteth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But nevertheless, be ye as those who watch, and so sleep, and rise again, and reign.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us watch now, let us sleep in death; at the end we shall rise again, and shall reign without end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The third thing we pray for is, that His will may be done as in heaven so in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in this too we wish well for ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the will of God must necessarily be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the will of God that the good should reign, and the wicked be damned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it possible that this will should not be done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what good do we wish for ourselves, when we say, “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this petition may be understood in many ways, and many things are to be in our thoughts in this petition, when we pray God, “Thy will be done as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As Thy Angels offend Thee not, so may we also not offend Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, how is “Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth,” understood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the holy Patriarchs, all the Prophets, all the Apostles, all the spiritual are as it were God’s heaven; and we in comparison of them are earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth;” as in them, so in us also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, “Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth;” the Church of God is heaven, His enemies are earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So we wish well for our enemies, that they too may believe and become Christians, and so the will of God be done, as in heaven, so also in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, “Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our spirit is heaven, and the flesh earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As our spirit is renewed by believing, so may our flesh be renewed by rising again; and “the will of God be done, as in heaven, so in earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, our mind whereby we see truth, and delight in this truth, is heaven; as, “I delight in the law of God, after the inward man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the earth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I see another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When this strife shall have passed away, and a full concord brought about of the flesh and spirit, the will of God will be done as in heaven, so also in earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we repeat this petition, let us think of all these things, and ask them all of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now all these things which we have mentioned, these three petitions, beloved, have respect to the life eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if the Name of our God is sanctified in us, it will be for eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If His kingdom come, where we shall live for ever, it will be for eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If His will be done as in heaven, so in earth, in all the ways which I have explained, it will be for eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There remain now the petitions for this life of our pilgrimage; therefore follows, “Give us this day our daily bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give us eternal things, give us things temporal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast promised a kingdom, deny us not the means of subsistence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wilt give everlasting glory with Thyself hereafter, give us in this earth temporal support.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore is it “day by day,” and “to-day,” that is, in this present time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when this life shall have passed away, shall we ask for daily bread then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then it will not be called, “day by day,” but “to-day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is called, “day by day,” when one day passes away, and another day succeeds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will it be called “day by day,” when there will be one eternal day?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This petition for daily bread is doubtless to be understood in two ways, both for the necessary supply of our bodily food, and for the necessities of our spiritual support.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a necessary supply of bodily food, for the preservation of our daily life, without which we cannot live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is food and clothing, but the whole is understood in a part.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we ask for bread, we thereby understand all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a spiritual food also which the faithful know, which ye too will know, when ye shall receive it at the altar of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This also is “daily Bread,” necessary only for this life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For shall we receive the Eucharist when we shall have come to Christ Himself, and begun to reign with Him for ever?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then the Eucharist is our daily bread; but let us in such wise receive it, that we be not refreshed in our bodies only, but in our souls.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the virtue which is apprehended there, is unity, that gathered together into His body, and made His members, we may be what we receive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then will it be indeed our daily bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, what I am handling before you now is “daily bread;” and the daily lessons which ye hear in church, are daily bread, and the hymns ye hear and repeat are daily bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all these are necessary in our state of pilgrimage.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when we shall have got to heaven, shall we hear the word, we who shall see the Word Himself, and hear the Word Himself, and eat and drink Him as the angels do now?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do the angels need books, and interpreters, and readers?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They read in seeing, for the Truth Itself they see, and are abundantly satisfied from that fountain, from which we obtain some few drops.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore has it been said touching our daily bread, that this petition is necessary for us in this life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is this necessary except in this life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the other we shall have no debts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what are debts, but sins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, ye are on the point of being baptized, then all your sins will be blotted out, none whatever will remain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatever evil ye have ever done, in deed, or word, or desire, or thought, all will be blotted out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet if in the life which is after Baptism there were security from sin, we should not learn such a prayer as this, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Only let us by all means do what comes next, “As we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye then who are about to enter in to receive a plenary and entire remission of your debts, do ye above all things see that ye have nothing in your hearts against any other, so as to come forth from Baptism secure, as it were free and discharged of all debts, and then begin to purpose to avenge yourselves on your enemies, who in time past have done you wrong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forgive, as ye are forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God can do no one wrong, and yet He forgiveth who oweth nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then ought he to forgive, who is himself forgiven, when He forgiveth all, who oweth nothing that can be forgiven Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Will this again be necessary in the life to come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation,” will not be said, except where there can be temptation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We read in the book of holy Job, “Is not the life of man upon earth a temptation?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then do we pray for?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle James saith, “Let no man say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He spoke of those evil temptations, whereby men are deceived, and brought under the yoke of the devil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the kind of temptation he spoke of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is another sort of temptation which is called a proving; of this kind of temptation it is written, “The Lord your God tempteth (proveth) you to know whether ye love Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means “to know”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “To make you know,” for He knoweth already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With that kind of temptation, whereby we are deceived and seduced, God tempteth no man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But undoubtedly in His deep and hidden judgment He abandons some.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when He hath abandoned them, the tempter finds his opportunity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he finds in him no resistance against his power, but forthwith presents himself to him as his possessor, if God abandon him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore that He may not abandon us, do we say, “Lead us not into temptation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For every one is tempted,” says the same Apostle James, “when he is drawn away of his own lust and enticed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then lust, when it hath conceived, bringeth forth sin; and sin, when it is finished, bringeth forth death.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then has he hereby taught us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To fight against our lusts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye are about to put away your sins in Holy Baptism; but lusts will still remain, wherewith ye must fight after that ye are regenerate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a conflict with your own selves still remains.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no enemy from without be feared: conquer thine own self, and the whole world is conquered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What can any tempter from without, whether the devil or the devil’s minister, do against thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever sets the hope of gain before thee to seduce thee, let him only find no covetousness in thee; and what can he who would tempt thee by gain effect?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas if covetousness be found in thee, thou takest fire at the sight of gain, and art taken by the bait of this corrupt food.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if he find no covetousness in thee, the trap remains spread in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or should the tempter set before thee some woman of surpassing beauty; if chastity be within, iniquity from without is overcome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore that he may not take thee with the bait of a strange woman’s beauty, fight with thine own lust within; thou hast no sensible perception of thine enemy, but of thine own concupiscence thou hast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not see the devil, but the object that engageth thee thou dost see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Get the mastery then over that of which thou art sensible within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fight valiantly, for He who hath regenerated thee is thy Judge; He hath arranged the lists, He is making ready the crown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because thou wilt without doubt be conquered, if thou have not Him to aid thee, if He abandon thee: therefore dost thou say in the prayer, “Lead us not into temptation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Judge’s wrath hath given over some to their own lusts; and the Apostle says, “God gave them over to the lusts of their hearts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did He give them up?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not by forcing, but by forsaking them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Deliver us from evil,” may belong to the same sentence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, that thou mayest understand it to be all one sentence, it runs thus, “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore he added “but,” to show that all this belongs to one sentence, “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will propose them singly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By delivering us from evil, He leadeth us not into temptation; by not leading us into temptation, He delivereth us from evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And truly it is a great temptation, dearly beloved, it is a great temptation in this life, when that in us is the subject of temptation, whereby we attain pardon, if in any of our temptations we have fallen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a frightful temptation, when that is taken from us, whereby we may be healed from the wounds of other temptations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know that ye have not yet understood me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me your attention, that ye may understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose avarice tempts a man, and he is conquered in any single temptation (for sometimes even a good wrestler and fighter may get roughly handled): avarice then has got the better of a man, good wrestler though he be, and he has done some avaricious act.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or there has been a passing lust; it has not brought the man to fornication, nor reached unto adultery, for when this does take place, the man must at all events be kept back from the criminal act.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he “hath seen a woman to lust after her;” he has let his thoughts dwell on her with more pleasure than was right; he has admitted the attack; excellent combatant though he be, he has been wounded, but he has not consented to it; he has beaten back the motion of his lust, has chastised it with the bitterness of grief, he has beaten it back; and has prevailed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Still in the very fact that he had slipped, has he ground for saying, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so of all other temptations, it is a hard matter that in them all there should not be occasion for saying, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is that frightful temptation which I have mentioned, that grievous, that tremendous temptation, which must be avoided with all our strength, with all our resolution; what is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we go about to avenge ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Anger is kindled, and the man burns to be avenged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O frightful temptation!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art losing that, whereby thou hadst to attain pardon for other faults.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou hadst committed any sin as to other senses, and other lusts, hence mightest thou have had thy cure, in that thou mightest say, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whoso instigateth thee to take vengeance, will lose for thee the power thou hadst to say, “As we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When that power is lost, all sins will be retained; nothing at all is remitted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our Lord and Master, and Saviour, knowing this dangerous temptation in this life, when He taught us six or seven petitions in this Prayer, took none of them for Himself to treat of, and to commend to us with greater earnestness, than this one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have we not said, “Our Father, which art in heaven;” and the rest which follows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why after the conclusion of the Prayer, did He not enlarge upon it to us, either as to what He had laid down in the beginning, or concluded with at the end, or placed in the middle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why said He not, if the Name of God be not hallowed in you, or if ye have no part in the kingdom of God, or if the will of God be not done in you, as in heaven, or if God guard you not, that ye enter not into temptation; why none of all these? but what saith He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you, that if ye forgive men their trespasses;” in reference to that petition, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Having passed over all the other petitions which He taught us, this He taught us with an especial force.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was no need of insisting so much upon those sins in which if a man offend, he may know the means whereby he may be cured: need of it there was, with regard to that sin in which if thou sin, there is no means whereby the rest can be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this thou oughtest to be ever saying, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is no lack of them; for we are but men; I have talked somewhat more than I ought, have said something I ought not, have laughed more than I ought, have eaten more than I ought, have listened with pleasure to what I ought not, have drunk more than I ought, have seen with pleasure what I ought not, have thought with pleasure on what I ought not; “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This if thou hast lost, thou art lost thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take heed, my brethren, my sons, sons of God, take heed, I beseech you, in that I am saying to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fight to the uttermost of your powers with your own hearts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if ye shall see your anger making a stand against you, pray to God against it, that God may make thee conqueror of thyself, that God may make thee conqueror, I say, not of thine enemy without, but of thine own soul within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He will give thee His present help, and will do it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would rather that we ask this of Him, than rain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye see, beloved, how many petitions the Lord Christ hath taught us; and there is scarce found among them one which speaks of daily bread, that all our thoughts may be moulded after the life to come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what can we fear that He will not give us, who hath promised and said, “Seek ye first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added unto you; for your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things before ye ask Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek ye first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For many have been tried even with hunger, and have been found gold, and have not been forsaken by God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They would have perished with hunger, if the daily inward bread were to leave their heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After this let us chiefly hunger.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “Blessed are they who hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He can in mercy look upon our infirmity, and see us, as it is said, “Remember that we are dust.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who from the dust made and quickened man, for that His work of clay’s sake, gave His Only Son to death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can explain, who can worthily so much as conceive, how much He loveth us?
